sn45.18:1.2: This pair feature up until SN 45.20. | With the rise of Pāṭaliputta, the “Chicken Monastery” (kukkuṭārāma) became an important center after the Buddha’s passing. Malalasekera (Dictionary of Pali Proper Names) suggests it was the same monastery later renowned as Aśokārāma. It seems that all the discourses set here are after the Parinibbana.
sn45.18:2.1: Notice that they refer to each other as “reverend”, in apparent violation of one of the Buddha’s final instructions (DN 16:6.2.1).
